two-pointers.title: Two Pointers
two-pointers.description: Use two indices moving through a sequence to eliminate redundant comparisons and achieve linear time.

variants.title: Inward Traversal
variants.description: Start from both ends and move pointers toward the center — ideal for sorted arrays and pairing problems.

variants.title: Unidirectional Traversal
variants.description: Both pointers move in the same direction at different speeds — common in linked lists and duplicate removal.

variants.title: Staged Traversal
variants.description: Process the array in phases with pointer resets — useful for multi-pass optimizations.

sliding-window.title: Sliding Window
sliding-window.description: Maintain a window of elements that slides across the array, updating incrementally instead of recomputing.

binary-search.title: Binary Search
binary-search.description: Repeatedly halve the search space on sorted or monotonic data to achieve logarithmic time complexity.

bfs.title: Breadth-First Search
bfs.description: Explore graphs level by level using a queue — ideal for shortest path and level-order traversal.

dfs.title: Depth-First Search
dfs.description: Dive deep into graph or tree structures using recursion or a stack before backtracking.

dynamic-programming.title: Dynamic Programming
dynamic-programming.description: Break problems into overlapping subproblems and cache results to avoid redundant computation.
